Based on the search results, here's what I found about BlueprintJS dark theme tokens customization:

## BlueprintJS Dark Theme Customization

### Applying Dark Theme
The dark theme in BlueprintJS is applied using the class `bp4-dark` (or `bp3-dark` in older versions).(no content)To add the dark theme to components like Popover2, you can add `popoverClassName="bp4-dark"` to the component.

### Dark Theme Style Variables
BlueprintJS provides Dark Theme Style Variables that are used to build custom UI components that should look similar to dark-themed BlueprintJS components.(no content)Some available dark theme variables include:
- `$pt-dark-dialog-box-shadow`
- `$pt-dark-input-box-shadow`
- `$pt-dark-popover-box-shadow`
- `$pt-dark-tooltip-box-shadow`

### Using the Variables
To use these variables in your SCSS, import them with:
```scss
@import "@blueprintjs/core/lib/scss/variables";
```

### Custom Theming with Blueprint Styler
There's a third-party tool called "blueprint-styler" that provides custom themes and styles for Palantir's Blueprint.js React component library.(no content)Dark theme switching is handled by CSS custom properties.(no content)You can override variables and styles by copy-pasting vars from files in `./src/styles/_blueprint/common/` and `./src/styles/_blueprint/components/`, and changing the values to override them.

### Official Documentation
Blueprint is an open source project developed at Palantir. The official documentation is available at [blueprintjs.com/docs](https://blueprintjs.com/docs/).

## BlueprintJS 6 Overview

Blueprint 6.0 focuses on the removal of deprecated and legacy code and the modernization of the codebase.

### Key Changes in Blueprint 6.0

- CSS namespace changed from bp5- to bp6-.
- React 16 and 17 are no longer supported as peer dependencies of Blueprint.
- @blueprintjs/datetime2 is now deprecated and should no longer be used. All component implementations have been merged into @blueprintjs/datetime.
- The react-day-picker dependency has changed from 7.4.9 to ^8.10.0.
- Component aliases like `<Navbar.Divider>`, `<Navbar.Group>`, and `<Navbar.Heading>` should be replaced with `<NavbarDivider>`, `<NavbarGroup>`, and `<NavbarHeading>` instead.

### Best Practices for Using Blueprint

Blueprint provides implementations for custom stylelint rules which enforce best practices for Blueprint usage.(no content)Blueprint UI Toolkit provides a wide range of components that you can use to build your user interface. These components are designed to be highly customizable and are built using React, making them easy to integrate into your existing React application.

**Styling Best Practices:**
- One of the key features of Blueprint UI Toolkit is its support for customizing styles. Blueprint provides a set of CSS variables that you can use to customize the appearance of components in your application. This allows you to create a consistent design system that matches your brand's visual identity.
- To customize the styles of Blueprint components, you can override the default CSS variables in your application. You can do this by creating a new CSS file and importing it into your project.

### Resources

- Blueprint is an open source project developed at Palantir.
- There's also a Figma design system modeled after Blueprint JS available in the Figma community for design work.

Here are the search results for BlueprintJS documentation on design tokens and theming:

## BlueprintJS Official Documentation

Blueprint is an open source project developed at Palantir.(no content)It's a React-based UI toolkit for the web.

### Official Resources

- **Main Documentation**: [blueprintjs.com/docs](https://blueprintjs.com/docs/)
- **GitHub Repository**: [github.com/palantir/blueprint](https://github.com/palantir/blueprint)

### Docs Theme Package

The `@blueprintjs/docs-theme` package is at version 6.0.10, last published 18 days ago. You can start using it by running `npm i @blueprintjs/docs-theme`.

### Theming with Custom Properties (Tokens)

For custom theming and design tokens support, there's a community project:

**[Blueprint Styler](https://github.com/pnnl/blueprint-styler)** - Blueprint uses global CSS to style its React components, allowing the styles to be overridden or replaced entirely. This project overrides the default Blueprint global CSS with a version that uses `--custom-properties` (also referred to as 'Tokens').(no content)You can import an existing style override, then in a `:root{...}` selector in your CSS, override `--custom-properties` from `blueprint-tokens.css`. If necessary, override global CSS in `blueprint.css` for additional control.

### Related: W3C Design Tokens Specification (2025)

The W3C Design Tokens specification reached its first stable version and addresses theming through standardized support for theming, modern color spaces, and cross-tool interoperability. It includes theming and multi-brand support to manage light/dark modes, accessibility variants, and brand themes without file duplication.

### Documentation Development

Much of Blueprint's documentation lives inside source code as JSDoc comments in `.tsx` files and KSS markup in `.scss` files. This documentation is extracted and converted into static JSON data using documentalist.

## BlueprintJS Dashboard and Data Visualization Resources

### Official Blueprint
Blueprint is a React-based UI toolkit for the web, developed as an open source project at Palantir.

### Dashboard Structure Patterns
For building dashboards with BlueprintJS, there are several recommended patterns:

**Navigation Setup:**
Using `Navbar` with `fixedToTop` keeps the header anchored during scrolling. `NavbarGroup` sections enable left/right content alignment through alignment props.

**Layout Structure:**
You can divide the central workspace into sidebar and visualization panel using Card components with a 12-column Grid system. For example, `span={2}` allocates 2/12 columns to the sidebar, and `elevation={2}` adds depth to the data panel. Nested Grid components enable responsive internal layouts.

**Tab Navigation:**
The Tabs component can be used with `vertical` and `renderActiveTabPanelOnly` props to implement collapsible menus for different data views like "Sales Data".

### Data Visualization Components
For data visualization, you can enable hover effects with ChartTooltip and wrap visualizations in ChartContainer for coordinated tooltips. The content prop formats tooltip data using the nearest datapoint.(no content)Charts can be bound to container dimensions using ResizeSensor combined with useState to dynamically update chart dimensions when parent elements resize. This hook pattern tracks container size changes and the chart redraws when dimensions update, maintaining aspect ratio constraints.

### Community Resources
There's community interest in seeing how people have used Blueprint components and patterns in different contexts. Users have requested collections of complete pages/websites built with Blueprint to serve as examples.

### General Dashboard Design Best Practices
An effective dashboard shows actionable and useful information at a glance. It simplifies the visual representation of complex data and helps stakeholders understand, analyze, and present key insights.(no content)Card layouts are very common for data dashboards. A card layout doesn't mean all charts are visually enclosed in a distinct "div" - it means charts and graphs are treated consistently, placed along their title, labels, legend and other accessories.

Based on the search results, here's information about BlueprintJS colors SCSS variables, palette, and intent colors:

## BlueprintJS Intent Colors

BlueprintJS defines intent colors using SCSS variables with default values:
- `$pt-intent-primary: $blue3 !default`
- `$pt-intent-success: $green3 !default`
- `$pt-intent-warning: $orange3 !default`
- `$pt-intent-danger: $red3 !default`

## Importing Variables

To use BlueprintJS SCSS variables in your project, import them with:
```scss
@import "@blueprintjs/core/lib/scss/variables";
```(no content)The `@import "~@blueprintjs/core/lib/scss/variables";` statement in Sass grants access to these layers.

## Color Package

The `@blueprintjs/colors` package contains color variables for Blueprint's color palette.

## Key Source Files

The relevant source files for BlueprintJS colors are:
- Blueprint `$pt-variables`: `packages/core/src/common/_variables.scss`
- Blueprint colors (`$blue3`, etc): `packages/core/src/common/_colors.scss`  
- Blueprint color aliases (`$pt-intent-primary`, etc): `packages/core/src/common/_color-aliases.scss`

## Overriding Intent Colors

When the `$Primary` intent color is overridden in SCSS, the hover states remain Blue because they are not related to the primary intent. The button intents are defined as:
```scss
$button-intents: (
  "primary": ($pt-intent-primary, $blue2, $blue1),
  "success": ($pt-intent-success, $green2, $green1),
  "warning": ($pt-intent-warning, $orange2, $orange1),
  "danger": ($pt-intent-danger, $red2, $red1)

Based on the search results, here's information about BlueprintJS colors SCSS variables, palette, and intent colors:

## BlueprintJS Intent Colors

BlueprintJS defines intent colors using SCSS variables with default values:
- `$pt-intent-primary: $blue3 !default`
- `$pt-intent-success: $green3 !default`
- `$pt-intent-warning: $orange3 !default`
- `$pt-intent-danger: $red3 !default`

## Importing Variables

To use BlueprintJS SCSS variables in your project, import them with:
```scss
@import "@blueprintjs/core/lib/scss/variables";
```(no content)The `@import "~@blueprintjs/core/lib/scss/variables";` statement in Sass grants access to these layers.

## Color Package

The `@blueprintjs/colors` package contains color variables for Blueprint's color palette.

## Key Source Files

The relevant source files for BlueprintJS colors are:
- Blueprint `$pt-variables`: `packages/core/src/common/_variables.scss`
- Blueprint colors (`$blue3`, etc): `packages/core/src/common/_colors.scss`  
- Blueprint color aliases (`$pt-intent-primary`, etc): `packages/core/src/common/_color-aliases.scss`

## Overriding Intent Colors

When the `$Primary` intent color is overridden in SCSS, the hover states remain Blue because they are not related to the primary intent. The button intents are defined as:
```scss
$button-intents: (
  "primary": ($pt-intent-primary, $blue2, $blue1),
  "success": ($pt-intent-success, $green2, $green1),
  "warning": ($pt-intent-warning, $orange2, $orange1),
  "danger": ($pt-intent-danger, $red2, $red1)
) !default;
```(no content)While CSS variables would be easier on end-users to override, it would be challenging to migrate to given the amount of sass math/operations happening with sass variables currently in BlueprintJS. You cannot call sass methods using CSS variables.

content.content:
# Blueprint Spacing System Migration: 10px to 4px

## Overview
Blueprint is transitioning from a 10px-based grid system (`$pt-grid-size`) to a 4px-based spacing system (`$pt-spacing`) to provide more flexible and consistent spacing options.

## New Spacing Scale
The 4px grid system uses these standardized values: **4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 32, 48**

These follow simple multiples that are easy to apply consistently throughout applications.

## Key Rationale

**Current limitations of 10px base:**
- Dividing 10px by 4 yields 2.5px, forcing rounding decisions that create visual inconsistencies
- Limited precision for dense information displays
- Misalignment with browser default font size (16px)

**Benefits of 4px base:**
- "The spacing scale follows simple multiples that are easy to remember and apply consistently"
- Provides fine-grained control without arbitrary pixel values
- Base-2 friendly calculations simplify responsive design
- Eliminates one-off spacing decisions

## Migration Path

### Variable Changes
```
// Deprecated approach
padding: $pt-grid-size * 0.5;  // 5px
margin: $pt-grid-size * 1.5;   // 15px

// New approach
padding: $pt-spacing;           // 4px
margin: $pt-spacing * 4;        // 16px
```

### Backward Compatibility
The `$pt-grid-size` variable remains available but is deprecated and will be removed in a future major release.

## Timeline
- **August 2025:** Introduction of `$pt-spacing`; deprecation notice for `$pt-grid-size`
- **September 2025:** Design validation, refinement, and component migration

# SCSS Variables from Blueprint Core

## Namespace & Grid
- `$ns: bp6`
- `$bp-ns: $ns`
- `$pt-grid-size: 10px` (legacy, 10px-based)
- `$pt-spacing: 4px` (primary spacing unit)

## Icons
- `$icons16-family: "blueprint-icons-16"`
- `$icons20-family: "blueprint-icons-20"`
- `$pt-icon-size-standard: $pt-spacing * 4`
- `$pt-icon-size-large: $pt-spacing * 5`

## Typography
- `$pt-font-family:` system fonts stack (-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, Roboto, Oxygen, Ubuntu, Cantarell, Open Sans, Helvetica Neue, icons family, sans-serif)
- `$pt-font-family-monospace: monospace`
- `$pt-font-size: $pt-spacing * 3.5`
- `$pt-font-size-large: $pt-spacing * 4`
- `$pt-font-size-small: $pt-spacing * 3`
- `$pt-line-height: 1.28581`

## Dimensions & Spacing
- `$pt-border-radius: 2px`
- `$pt-button-height: $pt-spacing * 7.5`
- `$pt-button-height-small: $pt-spacing * 6`
- `$pt-button-height-smaller: $pt-spacing * 5`
- `$pt-button-height-large: $pt-spacing * 10`
- `$pt-input-height: $pt-spacing * 7.5`
- `$pt-input-height-large: $pt-spacing * 10`
- `$pt-input-height-small: $pt-spacing * 6`
- `$pt-navbar-height: $pt-spacing * 12.5`

## Z-Indices
- `$pt-z-index-base: 0`
- `$pt-z-index-content: $pt-z-index-base + 10`
- `$pt-z-index-overlay: $pt-z-index-content + 10`
- `$pt-z-index-dialog-header: $pt-z-index-overlay + 10`

## Shadow Opacities
- `$pt-border-shadow-opacity: 0.1`
- `$pt-drop-shadow-opacity: 0.2`
- `$pt-dark-border-shadow-opacity: $pt-border-shadow-opacity * 2`
- `$pt-dark-drop-shadow-opacity: $pt-drop-shadow-opacity * 2`

## Elevation Shadows (Light Theme)
- `$pt-elevation-shadow-0` through `$pt-elevation-shadow-4` (multi-line shadow definitions)

## Elevation Shadows (Dark Theme)
- `$pt-dark-elevation-shadow-0` through `$pt-dark-elevation-shadow-4`

## Transitions
- `$pt-transition-ease: cubic-bezier(0.4, 1, 0.75, 0.9)`
- `$pt-transition-ease-bounce: cubic-bezier(0.54, 1.12, 0.38, 1.11)`
- `$pt-transition-duration: 100ms`

## Light Theme Box Shadows
- `$pt-input-box-shadow:` inset borders with black transparency
- `$pt-dialog-box-shadow: $pt-elevation-shadow-3`
- `$pt-popover-box-shadow: $pt-elevation-shadow-3`
- `$pt-tooltip-box-shadow: $pt-popover-box-shadow`
- `$pt-toast-box-shadow:` inset border shadow

## Dark Theme Box Shadows
- `$pt-dark-input-box-shadow:` inset borders with white transparency
- `$pt-dark-input-intent-box-shadow-colors:` map with primary, success, warning, danger keys
- `$pt-dark-dialog-box-shadow: $pt-dark-elevation-shadow-3`
- `$pt-dark-popover-border-color: hsl(215deg, 3%, 38%)`
- `$pt-dark-popover-box-shadow:` compound shadow
- `$pt-dark-tooltip-box-shadow:` drop shadows
- `$pt-dark-toast-box-shadow: $pt-dark-elevation-shadow-3`

## High Contrast Mode
- `$pt-high-contrast-mode-border-color: buttonborder`
- `$pt-high-contrast-mode-active-background-color: highlight`
- `$pt-high-contrast-mode-active-text-color: highlight`
- `$pt-high-contrast-mode-disabled-border-color: graytext`
- `$pt-high-contrast-mode-disabled-text-color: graytext`
- `$pt-high-contrast-mode-disabled-background-color: graytext`

Based on my search, here's a summary of Palantir design system and enterprise application best practices:

## Palantir Design System & Enterprise Application Best Practices

### Visual Hierarchy & Layout

A well-designed Workshop application interface guides a user's actions and maintains their attention, ensuring they can execute workflows in an efficient, effective, and logical manner.(no content)To ensure your application is intuitive to use, you should prioritize component clarity over complexity.(no content)To ensure product unification and consistency, you should create a cohesive experience across all applications so users feel they are working within a unified suite of tools.

### Avoiding Overload & Overcrowding

To avoid overload, limit the amount of total actionable items, such as tasks, notifications, or choices, per screen. Additionally, you should avoid showing more than five primary actions in your application's top-level navigation.(no content)To avoid overcrowding, maintain sufficient whitespace - ideally, this will comprise 30 to 40 percent of your screen. You should also limit the number of visible components to no more than ten in a single view, which includes buttons, panels, and widgets.

### Layout Best Practices

The landing or homepage centralizes all navigation activity and provides an overview of the application's embedded modules with custom thumbnails. A primary header maintains consistent logo design and global settings control across its embedded modules, most often located in the top-left and top-right corners of the page.(no content)Standardized layout elements ensure high-touch user components follow a consistent layout, such as positioning filtering widgets on the left side of a page and applying the same styles to other interface components, such as tabs, Metric Cards, headers, and buttons.(no content)Avoid horizontal scrolling whenever possible, except in cases where page-wide scrolling is necessary.

### Component-Specific Best Practices

**Tables and Lists:**
When adding Object Tables, Object Lists, or Pivot Tables to your application, always include counts to indicate the length of tables and lists by configuring a Metric Card widget in the table's section header.(no content)Ensure each section renders enough information for row selection; use collapsible detail panels for additional data instead of overlays, which can obstruct the base layer of information.

**Filtering:**
Use a Filter List's Vertical filter layout if the object list requires users to apply multiple filters simultaneously. This layout is also best used for complex filtering scenarios and provides a clear overview of available filtering options.(no content)Add an Exploration Search Bar to enable comprehensive searches with complex filtering capabilities. This is best suited for experienced users requiring detailed, precise querying functionality.

### Blueprint Design System (Open Source)

Blueprint is a newly open-sourced design system implemented as a collection of composable React components and optimized for desktop applications. It has changed the way we scale design and frontend engineering at Palantir by providing a systematic way to apply unified and consistent design across a diverse set of teams.(no content)Sass enables us to lay out the foundation of our visual design language as variables and functions. Changing a single Sass variable affects the entire system. This ensures visual consistency across the system and eases maintenance and iteration.

### Mobile Design Considerations

Mobile applications should usually be more tailored to the specific needs of your target user group than desktop applications. Each top-level page in your mobile application should be fairly simple, typically just allowing users to browse and filter a set of objects, then navigate to another page to view details and take action.(no content)When adding a Button Group widget in a mobile application, we recommend selecting the Large style option in most cases to ensure the button is easily selectable on a touchscreen. Where appropriate, we also recommend enabling the Fill available horizontal space option to further increase the size of the control target.

### Validation Methods

As you add sections and widgets to your application, we recommend practicing the following validation methods: Periodically squint at your interface to help identify the most important elements based on their intended use. This helps you determine the effectiveness of your hierarchy. Stay closely aligned with actual user workflows so you maintain an understanding of what users want to accomplish in each section of your interface.

Here's what I found about BlueprintJS Table component and data grid patterns for enterprise applications:

## BlueprintJS Table Component

The @blueprintjs/table package provides a scalable interactive table component. It offers a set of React components used to construct a highly configurable and performant table or spreadsheet.(no content)The latest version is 6.0.10, and there are 55 other projects in the npm registry using @blueprintjs/table.

### About BlueprintJS

BlueprintJS is a React-based UI toolkit for the web. This library is very optimized and popular for building interfaces that are complex data-dense for desktop applications.(no content)Blueprint is an open source project developed at Palantir.

### Basic Usage

The Table component allows users to display rows of data. The cellRenderer prop is used to define how data is displayed and can be set on each column component.(no content)The table package provides a scalable & interactive spreadsheet-like table component.

### Limitations for Enterprise Data Grids

Some developers have noted limitations when comparing BlueprintJS Table to specialized data grid libraries:

Row grouping is not available in the BlueprintJS library. It's similar to other general purpose GUI libraries like Bootstrap, Foundation, and Material-UI, so some developers don't consider it for specialized data grid use cases. Some prefer specialized data grid libraries like ag-Grid, Fancygrid, or Tabulator which provide rich features, including multi-level row grouping.(no content)There have been comments that the demo shows scrolling performance is not very good.

### Enterprise Data Grid Design Patterns

For complex enterprise data tables, if your table needs many features like sorting, filtering, pagination, nested rows, and editable content, it's called a "data grid" (or data tables in some design systems).(no content)AG Grid is considered "the mother of all table frameworks" for building excel-like functionality in the browser.(no content)When designing tables, it's important to note that generic advice provides design ideas, but users have specific needs. Doing user research to understand exactly what users need in those tables is recommended.

Based on my search, here's what I found about BlueprintJS React component examples and real-world applications:

## BlueprintJS Overview

BlueprintJS is a React-based UI toolkit for the web.(no content)This library is very optimized and popular for building interfaces that are complex data-dense for desktop applications.

## Examples and Resources

### Official Resources
- BlueprintJS publishes component libraries to NPM. The official documentation is available at [blueprintjs.com](https://blueprintjs.com/).

### Community Examples
- CodeSandbox has examples including "GoldenLayout Blueprintjs Table React" with a bug reproduction for blueprintjs table, and other BlueprintJS + React examples.
- StackBlitz has a Blueprint UI Sample as a starter project for React apps that exports to the create-react-app CLI.

### Real-World Projects Request
There's a GitHub issue (#2223) where users have asked if there's a collection of complete pages/websites built with Blueprint, noting "It would be really nice to see how people have used the components and patterns in different contexts."

## Component Examples

### Card Component
The Card Component is used as a simple rectangular container when the user wants to display content related to a single subject.(no content)The Card component takes two props: interactive and elevation. The elevation prop controls the shadow depth of the card, with higher values producing a more prominent shadow effect. The interactive prop accepts a boolean value. When set to true, it enables hover and click interactions on the card.

### Button Component
The Button component takes props such as intent, text, icon, small, and large. The intent prop defines the nature of the button, which reflects in its background color.

### Dialog Component
BlueprintJS provides a dialog component for modal interactions.

### Getting Started
Before using the components, include the CSS files from Blueprint UI Toolkit using imports for normalize.css, @blueprintjs/core/lib/css/blueprint.css, and @blueprintjs/icons/lib/css/blueprint-icons.css.

   252→### Zoom Thresholds
   253→
   254→| Layer | Zoom Range | Display |
   255→|-------|------------|---------|

   257→| MACRO → MESO | `0.35 - 0.50` | Crossfade zone |
   258→| MESO only | `0.50 - 2.00` | DNA/bars (100% opacity) |
   259→| MESO → MICRO | `2.00 - 2.15` | Crossfade zone |
   260→| MICRO only | `> 2.15` | Detailed cards (100% opacity) |

   262→### Configuration

   544→## BlueprintJS 6 Integration Notes
   545→
   546→- Use BlueprintJS 6 tokens where available (bp6-* CSS classes)
   547→- Override with `--mms-*` tokens for custom values
   548→- Cards use `Elevation.TWO` shadow via `<Card elevation={2}>`
   549→- Drawers use `<Drawer>` component, position on right for task details
   550→- Maintain dark theme via `bp6-dark` class on root
   551→- Use Intent colors: PRIMARY, SUCCESS, WARNING, DANGER
   552→
   553→### BlueprintJS Component Mapping
   554→
   555→| UI Need | BlueprintJS Component |
   556→|---------|----------------------|
   557→| Navbar | `<Navbar>` + `<NavbarGroup>` |
   558→| Sidebar | `<Card>` with elevation |
   559→| Buttons | `<Button>` / `<ButtonGroup>` |
   560→| Tags/Labels | `<Tag>` with intent |
   561→| Panels | `<Card>` / `<Drawer>` |
   562→| Inputs | `<InputGroup>` |

| **1-3 Months** | Weekly bars | Resource conflicts | Task redistribution |
| **2-4 Weeks** | Simplified Gantt | Track occupancy, bottlenecks | Block movement |
| **1 Week** | Detailed Gantt | Status per train, mileage | Fine scheduling |
| **1-3 Days** | Capsules with details | Fitness, remaining km | Specific route assignment |

---

## Wireframes (ASCII)

### Application Shell (Three-Layer Sandwich)

```
LAYER 1: BACKGROUND (Workers)
  - Constraint processing, availability calculations

LAYER 2: CANVAS (WebGL/R3F)
  - Task bars, dependency lines, heatmaps
  - LOD rendering (Macro/Meso/Micro)

LAYER 3: HUD OVERLAY (HTML/React)
  - pointer-events: none (except buttons)
  - Time axis header
  - Train list sidebar
  - Tooltips and context menus
  - Scenario sidebar
```

### Train Capsule Component

```
+-----------------------------------+
| [train] KS-042  |  B-Check  | 3d  |
| Kinki Sharyo    [======--]        |
+-----------------------------------+
  ^        ^          ^        ^

  |        |          +----------- Maintenance type badge
  |        +---------------------- Train ID (monospace)
  +------------------------------- Fleet icon
```

### Financial Pulse HUD

```

|                            |
| Baseline: AED 12.4M        |
| Current:  AED 12.7M (+2.4%)|
|                            |

+----------------------------+
```
Position: Top-right corner, always visible

### Zoom Level Indicator

```

|                                                                     |
| View: [Workload Equalizer v] [Gantt] [Calendar] [Matrix]           |
|       ------------------                                            |
|            (default)                                                |
|                                                                     |
| Mode: [* Baseline] [o Scenario: "Strike Impact"]                   |
+---------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Scenario Panel

```
+---------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                               |
|  [PRODUCTION *]    [SCENARIO o]                              |
|                                                               |
|  +-----------------------------------------------------------+
|  | Scenarios:                                                 |
|  |  * "Baseline 2025" (current)                              |
|  |  * "August Strike Impact" <- ACTIVE                       |
|  |  * "Fleet Expansion +20 trains"                           |
|  |  [+ New Scenario]                                         |
|  +-----------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                               |
|  Delta from Baseline:                                         |
|   * OPEX 2026: +$2.3M (+4.2%)                                |
|   * Overruns: 12 trains at risk                              |
|   * Man-hours Q3: -2,400 (strike)                            |
|                                                               |
|  [Discard] [Compare with...] [Apply to Production]            |
|                                                               |
+---------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Minimap

```
+----------------------------------+
|  [Heatmap Grid 40x10]            |
|  +----+                          |
|  |    | <- Viewport indicator    |
|  +----+                          |
+----------------------------------+
```
Position: Bottom-right, always visible
Dimensions: 200px x 80px

### LOD Transition Zones

```
Zoom:  0.05  0.35  0.50         2.00  2.15  5.0
        |     |     |             |     |    |
MACRO   [=====|-----|             |     |    ]  100% -> fade out
MESO          [-----|=============|-----|    ]  fade in -> 100% -> fade out
MICRO               |             [-----|====]  fade in -> 100%
        |     |     |             |     |    |
        |     +-----+             +-----+    |
        |   crossfade            crossfade   |
        |     zone                 zone      |
```

### Component Directory Structure

```
src/components/
+-- layout/           # AppHeader, Sidebar, TimeScrubber
+-- controls/         # ZoomLevelIndicator, ViewSelector, ModeToggle
+-- common/           # MetricBadge, TaskTooltip, ConflictAlert
+-- gantt/            # TaskBlock, GanttRow, TimelineHeader
+-- heatmap/          # HeatmapCell, HeatmapGrid, DNAStrip
+-- canvas/           # R3F wrapper components
    +-- HeatmapCanvas.tsx
    +-- GanttCanvas.tsx
    +-- DNAStripCanvas.tsx

src/render/           # R3F/Three.js rendering
+-- layers/
    +-- MacroLayer.ts
    +-- MesoLayer.ts
    +-- MicroLayer.ts
+-- FractalRenderer.ts
```

### Fractal Propagation (Bidirectional Changes)

```
OPERATIONAL CHANGE (Day level):
  +-- Change train 104 route: Long -> Short
       +-- Daily mileage changes: -450km
            +-- Next PM prediction: +3 days
                 +-- March workload changes
                      +-- Q1/2026 workload changes
                           +-- 2026 annual cost changes
                                +-- 12-year projection changes

STRATEGIC CHANGE (12-year level):
  +-- Move Alstom fleet overhaul: 2027 -> 2028
       +-- Budget 2027 decreases, Budget 2028 increases
            +-- Monthly workload redistributes
                 +-- Weekly track availability changes
                      +-- Daily scheduling needs recalculation
                           +-- Individual train routes may conflict
```

---

## BlueprintJS 6 Integration Notes

- Use BlueprintJS 6 tokens where available (bp6-* CSS classes)
- Override with `--mms-*` tokens for custom values
- Cards use `Elevation.TWO` shadow via `<Card elevation={2}>`
- Drawers use `<Drawer>` component, position on right for task details
- Maintain dark theme via `bp6-dark` class on root
- Use Intent colors: PRIMARY, SUCCESS, WARNING, DANGER

### BlueprintJS Component Mapping

| UI Need | BlueprintJS Component |
|---------|----------------------|
| Navbar | `<Navbar>` + `<NavbarGroup>` |
| Sidebar | `<Card>` with elevation |
| Buttons | `<Button>` / `<ButtonGroup>` |
| Tags/Labels | `<Tag>` with intent |
| Panels | `<Card>` / `<Drawer>` |
| Inputs | `<InputGroup>` |
| Selects | `<Select>` / `<HTMLSelect>` |
| Tooltips | `<Tooltip>` |
| Toasts | `<OverlayToaster>` |

---

## Performance Considerations

- Canvas background: Pure black (`#0A0A0A`) for OLED power savings
- Minimize color variety in heatmaps to improve GPU batching
- Use opacity transitions over color transitions for smoother LOD
- Prefer `transform` and `opacity` for animations (GPU-accelerated)

The library has evolved with a new spacing system (4px base), updated CSS namespace (`bp6-`), and modernized codebase requiring React 18+. --- ## 1. Official Resources | Resource | URL | |----------|-----| | Official Documentation | [blueprintjs.com/docs](https://blueprintjs.com/docs/) | | GitHub Repository | [github.com/palantir/blueprint](https://github.com/palantir/blueprint) | | Blueprint 6.0 Migration Guide | [Blueprint 6.0 Wiki](https://github.com/palantir/blueprint/wiki/Blueprint-6.0) | | Figma Design System | [Blueprint JS Figma Community](https://www.figma.com/community/file/1178851446892338192/blueprint-js-design-system) | | Colors Package | [@blueprintjs/colors on NPM](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@blueprintjs/colors) | --- ## 2. Spacing System According to the [Spacing System Migration wiki](https://github.com/palantir/blueprint/wiki/Spacing-System-Migration:-10px-to-4px): ### 4.1 New 4px Grid System Blueprint has transitioned from a 10px base (`$pt-grid-size`) to a **4px base** (`$pt-spacing`). **Spacing Scale**: `4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 32, 48` (all multiples of 4) ### 4.2 Benefits of 4px System - More flexible spacing options - Base-2 friendly (simplifies responsive design) - Eliminates fractional pixel values - Better precision for dense information displays ### 4.3 Variable Migration ```scss // OLD (deprecated) padding: $pt-grid-size * 0.5; // 5px - creates subpixel issues margin: $pt-grid-size * 1.5; // 15px // NEW (recommended) padding: $pt-spacing; // 4px margin: $pt-spacing * 4; // 16px ``` ### 4.4 Key Dimension Variables From [_variables.scss](https://github.com/palantir/blueprint/blob/develop/packages/core/src/common/_variables.scss): ```scss $ns: bp6; // CSS namespace $pt-spacing: 4px; // Base spacing unit $pt-grid-size: 10px; // DEPRECATED // Icons $pt-icon-size-standard: $pt-spacing * 4; // 16px $pt-icon-size-large: $pt-spacing * 5; // 20px // Typography $pt-font-size: $pt-spacing * 3.5; // 14px $pt-font-size-large: $pt-spacing * 4; // 16px $pt-font-size-small: $pt-spacing * 3; // 12px $pt-line-height: 1.28581; // Component Heights $pt-button-height: $pt-spacing * 7.5; // 30px $pt-button-height-small: $pt-spacing * 6; // 24px $pt-button-height-smaller: $pt-spacing * 5; // 20px $pt-button-height-large: $pt-spacing * 10; // 40px $pt-input-height: $pt-spacing * 7.5; // 30px $pt-input-height-large: $pt-spacing * 10; // 40px $pt-input-height-small: $pt-spacing * 6; // 24px $pt-navbar-height: $pt-spacing * 12.5; // 50px // Border & Radius $pt-border-radius: 2px; // Transitions $pt-transition-ease: cubic-bezier(0.4, 1, 0.75, 0.9); $pt-transition-ease-bounce: cubic-bezier(0.54, 1.12, 0.38, 1.11); $pt-transition-duration: 100ms; ``` --- ## 5. Typography System ### 5.1 Font Stack ```scss $pt-font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Oxygen, Ubuntu, Cantarell, "Open Sans", "Helvetica Neue", $icons16-family, sans-serif; $pt-font-family-monospace: monospace; ``` ### 5.2 Typography Best Practices From the official documentation: 1. **Base font size**: 14px (default for most short strings) 2. **Line height**: 1.28581 (optimal for UI text) 3. **Running text**: Use `.pt-running-text` for longform content (Markdown, docs) 4. **UI text reset**: Use `.pt-ui-text` to reset to default styles 5. **Monospace**: Use `.bp6-monospace-text` for train IDs, codes, timestamps 6. **WCAG Compliance**: Default text colors meet WCAG 2.0 minimum contrast ### 5.3 Typography Classes | Class | Usage | |-------|-------| | `.bp6-ui-text` | Reset to default UI text styles | | `.bp6-running-text` | Longform content (increased line-height) | | `.bp6-monospace-text` | Code, IDs, timestamps | | `.bp6-text-large` | Large text variant | | `.bp6-text-small` | Small text variant | | `.bp6-text-muted` | Muted/secondary text | | `.bp6-text-disabled` | Disabled state text | --- ## 6.
